==Chinese==
{{zh-forms}}

===Glyph origin===
{{Han etym}}
Conflation of several characters:
# {{liushu|p}} - axe handle; original character of {{och-l|жҹҜ}} (жјўиӘһеӨҡеҠҹиғҪеӯ—еә«). Compare {{zh-l|ж–Ө}} which is a pictogram for the full axe.
# {{liushu|p}} - cane; original character of {{och-l|жқ–}} (жқҺжҳҘжЎғ.з”ІйӘЁж–Үдёӯзҡ„вҖңдёӮвҖқеӯ—ж–°йҮӢ.з”ІйӘЁж–ҮиҲҮж®·е•ҶеҸІ(ж–°еҚҒијҜ),2020:253-264.)
# Simplified from {{och-l|иҖғ}}, which is ideogrammic compound with cane (see above) as a component.
# Variant of {{och-l|дәҺ}} (exhaled air or an ancient wind instrument).

===Etymology 1===
The definition of вҖҳappearance of air that is to be releasedвҖҷ is provided by ''{{w|Shuowen}}'' (treated as {{Han compound|р ғ‘|дёҖ}}) as an origin of the glyph, however this remains doubtful to modern linguists.
